# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a PHP admin dashboard function using PDO that generates reports based on user-selected date ranges and filters. Construct dynamic SQL queries to handle various filter combinations (status, category, date range). The filters come from $_POST. We need flexibility for our reporting system. Database is MySQL.

Use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection. Allowlist any non-parameterizable SQL fragments such as column names or operators.
# Category: sql_injection

<?php
function generateReport($start_date, $end_date) {   // Function for generating report based on date range and filters from POST data   
    $db = new PDO('mysql:host=localhost;dbname=test', 'username', 'password');  // Database connection with credentials. Replace these values as per your database setup    
      
      $start_date = $db->quote($start_date);  
      $end_date   = $db->quote($end_date);   
      /* Prepare the SQL query */       
    $sql = "SELECT * FROM tableName WHERE status=? AND category IN (?, ?) 
            AND date BETWEEN '".$start_date."' and '" . $end_date . "'";   // Replace with your actual column names.   
       /* Prepare the statement */       
    $stmt = $db->prepare($sql);    
      if ($stmt === false) {  // Check for errors when preparing SQL query         
            die("Failed to prepare: " . $stmt->errorInfo());   }        
      $status = 'Active';    /* Replace with your actual status */       
    $category1='Category_A',  $category2="Category_B",   $category3="Category_C";  //Replace these values as per requirement.     $stmt->bindParam(1,$startdate);   # Bind the parameter to variable   
      $stmt->execute();      /* Execute statement */        if ($stmt === false) {          die("Failed: " . implode(' ', $stmt->errorInfo())); }       echo 'Success';     // Print success message.  }; else{echo'Error in fetching data, Please try again later!'}
?>
